response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c5 is in critical distress: islanded with SoC at 12.5% (0.25/2 kWh) and a 1.0 kW load consuming the battery at an unsustainable rate. At this depletion trajectory, the household has approximately 15 minutes of autonomy remaining. The memory log shows a consistent pattern of rejections from all peers (r0c5, r1c4, r2c5) over the past 45 minutes, citing r1c5's own critically low SoC as the barrier to receiving aid. This creates a deadlock: r1c5 cannot share (SoC too low), cannot receive (peers also conservative/stressed), and cannot reconnect to grid (islanded=True with no reconnection signal). The absence of any trust circles means no priority override exists. The only viable path forward is aggressive load shedding to extend battery life while signaling urgent need to the geographic circle and attempting grid reconnection. Continuing at 1.0 kW load will result in complete depletion within minutes.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.80
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "CRITICAL: SoC 12.5% with 1.0 kW load = ~15 min autonomy. Islanded deadlock detected—all peers rejecting due to own low reserves. No trust circles. Immediate actions required: (1) shed non-critical load to <0.3 kW, (2) broadcast URGENT reconnection request to grid operator, (3) halt all outbound sharing until SoC >0.80. Geographic peers (r0c5, r2c5) show declining SoC trend; reciprocal aid unlikely within critical window. Self-preservation mode mandatory."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
